_doc: Performance baselines and gates for projected-observers. See docs/TEST_PERFORMANCE.md for the full policy. Two layers: fast_perf_gates run in normal pytest; production_baselines are opt-in (--perf-long) and require explicit capture runs (tests/perf/capture_m7b_baseline.py).

m8_quick_cuda_batched._description: Default automatic gate. Runs in normal pytest invocations. Catches obvious regressions in CA stepping, batched response-map dispatch, summary writers, joblib worker init, and the CLI plumbing for --backend cuda-batched.
m8_quick_cuda_batched.config: M8 --quick --backend cuda-batched (3 rules per source x 2 seeds x T=80 x 16x16x4x4)
m8_quick_cuda_batched._observed_note: Empirically ~8.5s on the captured machine; gate at 15.0s absorbs numba JIT cold-start variance and joblib spawn overhead in fresh subprocess. min_candidates=10 catches the silent-failure mode where every cell errors out (wall time alone passes when nothing actually runs).

m8_m7b_class_numpy._description: M8 mechanism discovery at M7B-class production scale on the slow numpy backend. The reference point for measuring cuda-batched speedup on the same config. Captured opportunistically during the perf refactor; not run in normal pytest.
m8_m7b_class_numpy._post_sweep_note: Original run's summary.md write failed mid-stream on a UnicodeEncodeError (cp1252 cannot encode U+2192 right-arrow). Recovered manually via the encoding=utf-8 fix in commit cbc0a79. CSVs, stats JSON, and plots all wrote correctly during the original run; only summary.md was empty before recovery.

tolerance_policy._description: When a re-run is checked against a captured baseline: if its wall_time_seconds exceeds the baseline by more than warn_regression_fraction (20%), the test emits a warning. With --perf-gate, that warning becomes a hard assertion failure.

rerun_commands._doc: Run from the repository root with the .venv activated.
